assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and email address.
No Square account or Developer Console application exists yet.
Sandbox CreatePayment is acceptable; production activation is a later gate.

developer_goal: Create a Square developer account and application, then complete the official Get Started path to the first successful Sandbox payment verification.

first_success.outcome: Unresolved between API Explorer CreatePayment status COMPLETED and the seller Dashboard Verify the Payment step.
first_success.observable_signal: Make your First API Call treats COMPLETED as success; the four-step Get Started sequence continues through Verify the Payment in the seller Dashboard.

route_selection.surface: Square Developer signup and Developer Console Get Started
route_selection.rule: Use the documented self-service Get Started sequence. Do not invent a winner when the terminal verification step is ambiguous.

candidates.name: Get Started through CreatePayment COMPLETED in API Explorer
candidates.reason: This is the first authenticated payment success signal, but the platform journey still lists Verify the Payment as a later numbered step.

candidates.name: Get Started through seller Dashboard payment verification
candidates.reason: Matches the official four-step page order, but adds Dashboard navigation and date-filter interaction after the API already succeeded.

candidates.name: SDK language quickstarts instead of API Explorer
candidates.reason: Get Started's first call path uses API Explorer with a prefilled Sandbox token rather than a language SDK sample.

required_path.action: Complete Square Developer sign-up and register the first application.

required_fields.label: Terms, Privacy Policy, and E-Sign Consent

required_path.observable_result: Square opens the new application in the Developer Console.

required_path.action: Open the prepopulated CreatePayment request in API Explorer from the application banner.
required_path.observable_result: API Explorer shows a Sandbox CreatePayment request with access token prefilled.

required_path.action: Choose Run Request and confirm the Payment response status is COMPLETED.
required_path.observable_result: Square returns a Payment object with status COMPLETED.

required_path.action: If the selected terminal includes seller verification, open the Sandbox seller Dashboard and locate the test payment.
required_path.observable_result: The test payment is visible in the seller-facing Sandbox Dashboard.

external_gates.description: Taking payments in production requires activating the Square account at squareup.com/activation.

platform_outcomes.description: Sign-up creates the developer account and registers the first application.
API Explorer prefills Sandbox access token and CreatePayment fields for the Get Started exercise.

excluded.item: Optional signup preference questions for samples and help content
excluded.reason: Official create-account docs label preferences optional.
Documented only when ready to take live payments, after Sandbox Get Started.

sources.title: Get Started
evidence_supported: Create account through Verify the Payment

sources.title: View the API Logs
evidence_supported: API Logs step in Get Started sequence

sources.title: Verify the Payment
evidence_supported: Seller Dashboard verification as final Get Started step

uncertainties.question: Does first success end at CreatePayment COMPLETED, or must Verify the Payment in the seller Dashboard also complete?
uncertainties.impact: Including Dashboard verification adds actions after the API already succeeded and changes counts.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Human judgment on the corpus terminal against the four-step Get Started page versus the COMPLETED response wording.

uncertainties.question: Are View the API Logs required before first success, or only instructional?
uncertainties.impact: The middle Get Started step may or may not be a required developer action.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Clarify whether logs are mandatory confirmation or optional observation after COMPLETED.

uncertainties.question: Do current signup prompts add fields beyond the documented name, email, password, country, agreements, business name, and application name?
uncertainties.impact: Field counts could rise if anti-abuse or email verification screens appear.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account Developer signup walkthrough.
